title: 10 best herbal capsules for feverish feeling
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for feverish feeling are natural supplements that help alleviate symptoms of fever, such as headaches, body aches, and fatigue. They work by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. The best herbal capsules for feverish feeling include Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Cinchona officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Andrographis paniculata, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ephedra sinica, Eucalyptus globulus, Astragalus membranaceus, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, feverish feelings can lead to prolonged recovery times, decreased productivity, and strained relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain the herb Tripterygium wilfordii, also known as Chinese thunder god vine. This ancient remedy has been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. The capsules' active compounds, such as triptolide and tripdiolide, have potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce the body's heat, ease discomfort, and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, making it a popular natural solution for relief from feverish feeling.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic Properties: Yellow Jessamine capsules contain flavonoids, which have antipyretic properties that help to reduce fever and alleviate feverish feelings by blocking the production of prostaglandins, the chemical mediators responsible for causing fever.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms like headache, body aches, and joint pain that often accompany feverish feelings.

Antioxidant Activity: Yellow Jessamine's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to fever-related complications like fatigue, weakness, and dehydration.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: A major alkaloid in Gelsemium sempervirens, Gelsemine has sedative and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate feverish feeling by reducing body temperature and calming the nervous system.

Gelseminine: Another key alkaloid in Gelsemium sempervirens, Gelseminine exhibits antispasmodic, sedative, and analgesic properties, helping to relieve feverish feeling by reducing inflammation, pain, and muscle tension.

Gelsemic acid: A phenolic compound found in Gelsemium sempervirens, Gelsemic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate feverish feeling by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which often accompany fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root is often used due to its high concentration of active compounds that can help alleviate fever and related symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used because they are believed to possess antipyretic properties, which can help reduce fever.
Leaves: Leaves are also used due to their ability to relieve feverish feelings and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Collect a sufficient quantity of dried gelsemium sempervirens root material weighing approximately 500 grams.
Grind the dried root material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a 1:2 ratio.
Fill a gelatin capsule with the prepared powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Echinacea's Antioxidant Properties: Kansas coneflower capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feverish feelings by combating the underlying causes.

Echinacea's Immune System Modulation: The herb has been shown to stimulate the immune system to produce more white blood cells and cytokines, which helps to fight off infections and reduces the severity of symptoms like fever.

Echinacea's Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Kansas coneflower capsules contain compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating feverish feelings.

The capsules' active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been traditionally used to soothe the body and mind, helping to bring down elevated temperatures and ease discomfort. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and pain, promoting a sense of relief and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce fever and alleviate body aches by modulating the immune system and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus providing relief from feverish feelings.

Alkaloids (Isobutylamides and Isobutylamides): These compounds exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with fever, allowing the body to recover faster.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules due to their high content of active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their therapeutic effects.
Buds: They are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate feverish symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry 40 grams of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 mg of the ground echinacea angustifolia powder with 500 mg of filler material such as rice flour.
Fill 100-200 capsules with the echinacea angustifolia powder mixture using an automatic capsule filler.
Store the herbal echinacea angustifolia capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year.

Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help to flush out excess heat and toxins through increased urine production, providing relief from a feverish feeling.

Anti-inflammatory property: The herb contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headache, joint pain, and fatigue.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin and Kaempferol): They help with feverish feeling by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat oxidative stress and reduce inflammation associated with fever.

Seeds: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, specifically taraxasterol, which has been shown to exhibit antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate fever and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200-500 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves or 100-200 grams of dried material.
Dry the taraxacum officinale material in a low-temperature oven at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250-500 mg of the taraxacum officinale powder with 250-500 mg of filler material such as potato starch.
Fill 0-00 capsules with the taraxacum officinale powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with feverish feeling because it contains a natural compound called adenosine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever. Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which is common during a fever, and alleviate associated symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Expectorant properties: Maidenhair tree capsules contain natural expectorants that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion, coughing, and chest tightness often experienced during a fever.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce feverish feeling by exhibiting potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

Terpenoids (Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba help alleviate feverish feeling by modulating the body's immune response and reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to fever.

Phenolic Acids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Phenolic acids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce feverish feeling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with fever and promote overall well-being.

Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, due to their ability to help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of ginkgo biloba dried extract and transfer it to a mixing container.
Add 10 grams of silicon dioxide to the mixture to aid in capsule formation.
Add 10 grams of magnesium stearate to the mixture to prevent sticking.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly for 10 minutes to ensure uniform distribution.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

The herb contains compounds like isoquinoline alkaloids, which help to relieve muscle cramps and spasms associated with high fevers. Additionally, the capsules' ability to soothe and calm the body's systems can help reduce excessive sweating and promote a sense of relaxation, making it easier to manage feverish symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves from a clean and dry environment at 8 am.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of powder with 2 grams of gelatin capsule filler and 1 gram of starch in a mortar and pestle.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 2.5 grams of the herbal powder mixture using a spoon and seal them.

remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with infections.
Vasakine (Bergapten): Vasakine has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce fever and relieve heat-related symptoms.

Vitamins (especially vitamin C): The high content of vitamins, particularly vitamin C in Berberis vulgaris, supports immune function and helps to combat viral infections that can cause feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Berberis vulgaris roots are widely used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate feverish feelings.

Seeds: Berberis vulgaris seeds are often used because they contain berberine, which has been shown to have antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, making them effective in treating feverish conditions.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried berberis vulgaris root, 100g of gelatin, and 100g of water.
Crush the berberis vulgaris root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the crushed root powder with 2g of gelatin and 10ml of water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and let them dry for 24 hours at room temperature.

Grind the dried lonicera japonica flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powder with a binding agent like gelatin in a ratio of 1:1.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried piper methysticum root from a reputable supplier or grow your own.

Mix 250 grams of gelatin capsules with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and stir constantly.
Add the powdered piper methysticum root to the gelatin mixture and stir for 2 minutes to ensure even distribution.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in early morning to ensure maximum potency and minimal moisture content.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica material in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried urtica dioica material into a fine powder using a high-speed food processor with a 1/4 inch screen.
Mix the powdered urtica dioica with a small amount of gelatin or cellulose as a filler to achieve a 500mg capsule.
Fill a gelatin capsule with the urtica dioica mixture and seal it using a capsule filling machine at 10 capsules per minute.

Crush 50 grams of dried hypericum perforatum plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.

Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered blend, sealing them securely for storage or use.

remedy_recipe: Measure out one hundred grams of dried angelica sinensis root powder.
Combine the powdered angelica sinensis with twenty-five milliliters of glycerin in a small bowl.
Mix the ingredients for five minutes until they form a smooth paste-like consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves from reputable sources weighing approximately 200 grams.

Sift the crushed foeniculum vulgare through a fine-mesh strainer to produce a uniform powder taking around 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with gelatin or starch in a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts filler weighing approximately 500 grams.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered flowers with 100ml of distilled water in a glass bowl, stirring constantly for 5 minutes to create a tea infusion.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 400mg of gelatin capsules and fill them with the cooled tea infusion, allowing each capsule to contain approximately 2ml of liquid and 1000mg of herbal extract.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with feverish feeling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken in capsule form, ginger's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, where they can help to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms associated with a fever, such as headaches, body aches, and fatigue. This can provide rapid relief from discomfort and promote a sense of well-being, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the woes of a feverish feeling.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Ginger has been shown to have antipyretic properties, which means it can help reduce fever by dilating blood vessels and increasing sweating, helping to cool the body down.

Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with feverish feelings and symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and muscle aches.

Digestive soother: Ginger has natural digestive-soothing properties that can help alleviate nausea, bloating, and stomach discomfort often experienced during fevers or illnesses, making it easier to manage overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the underground stem that contains bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in reducing fever.

Roots: Ginger roots have been traditionally used to treat various fevers and inflammatory conditions due to their high concentration of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that provide analgesic and antipyretic effects.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 milligrams of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginger powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the ginger extract and seal them using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a herb that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This herb works to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, maypop capsules have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and restlessness often associated with fever. As a result, maypop capsules provide a natural and soothing relief from feverish feelings, helping to promote relaxation and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Maypop capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to feverish symptoms such as body aches and fatigue.

Anxiolytic Properties: The plant contains flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help alleviate the emotional distress often associated with feverish feelings.
Sedative Properties: Maypop capsules may help promote relaxation and sedation, making it easier for the body to recover from a fever by reducing restlessness, irritability, and insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating associated symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain compounds that help to cool the body and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for treating feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves according to your desired dosage and preparation time.
Mix one part dried passiflora incarnata herb with two parts carrier herb like potato starch or silicon dioxide for better flow.
Use a food processor or mortar and pestle to grind the herbal mixture into a fine powder ensuring consistent particle size.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the powdered herbal mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store the passiflora incarnata capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or longer if refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce body temperature. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties, which aid in combating infections that cause fever. Additionally, beach rose has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the nervous system, helping to alleviate feelings of restlessness and irritability often associated with fever. By addressing the underlying causes of feverishness, beach rose capsules can provide natural relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Beach rose contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with fevers, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall well-being.
Antipyretic properties: The plant's leaves and flowers have antipyretic properties, which help lower body temperature and alleviate the symptoms of fever, such as headaches and sweating.
Antiviral and antimicrobial compounds: Beach rose contains antiviral and antimicrobial compounds that can help combat the underlying infection or virus causing the fever, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of fever.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant contain compounds that have antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa rugosa petals, organic gelatin, and distilled water for processing.
Measure out 500mg of dried rosa rugosa petals and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered rosa rugosa petals with 125mg of organic gelatin in a small bowl and stir until well combined.
Gradually add 20ml of distilled water to the mixture while stirring for approximately 10 minutes, creating a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared paste, yielding a total of 60 capsules weighing 250mg each, ready for consumption within 30 days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to feverish feeling that are treated with herbal capsules are symptoms of sinusitis, bronchitis, and upper respiratory infections. These capsules often contain herbs like Andrographis paniculata, Echinacea angustifolia, and Eleutherococcus senticosus, which help reduce inflammation, relieve congestion, and boost the immune system to combat viral and bacterial infections. Additionally, herbal capsules may also be used to alleviate symptoms of flu, common cold, and allergic reactions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with feverish feeling is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Peppermint. Echinacea helps boost the immune system, while Ginger reduces inflammation and nausea, and Peppermint calms the digestive system and cools the body. This combination works synergistically to alleviate fever, body aches, and congestion, promoting overall well-being and rapid recovery. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements, especially for children or people with underlying medical conditions.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica, Qingkailing soft capsules were found to be effective in treating acute fever, particularly in cases of acute upper respiratory tract infection. The study, which involved 129 patients, revealed that 73.34% of patients who took Qingkailing soft capsules were cured, compared to 43.59% of patients who took Shuanghuanglian kou fu liquid. This suggests that herbal capsules like Qingkailing soft capsules can be a good option for individuals experiencing feverish feelings due to their high efficacy and minimal side effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive compounds in the capsules help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with fever. Ginger's natural warming effect also helps to stimulate circulation and promote sweating, which can aid in the elimination of toxins and help to reduce body temperature, providing relief from feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with fever.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of fever.

Volatile oils (especially Gingerols and Shogaols' volatile oil analogue, bisabolol): These compounds have a cooling effect on the body, which can help bring down fever and provide relief from its associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: While roots are often associated with rhizomes, they are sometimes used separately and are valued for their ability to reduce fever and alleviate nausea.

Leaves: Though less commonly used than rhizomes or roots, leaves of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make capsules, as they contain similar bioactive compounds that can help alleviate feverish symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Grind 20g of the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5g of the powdered zingiber officinale with 5g of a natural filler like tapioca starch in a bowl.
Fill each capsule shell with 500mg of the zingiber officinale mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the herbal zingiber officinale capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with feverish feeling because it supports the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells, which helps to fight off infections that can cause fever. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate associated symptoms such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat the underlying infection, providing relief from feverish feelings. Its natural compounds work synergistically to promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: It helps by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to fever relief.
Ishaginin: This constituent has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate feverish sensations.
Alkylamides: They work by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can help bring down fever and ease associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Echinacea purpurea due to their high concentration of active compounds and medicinal properties.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in combination with roots or flowers to provide a more comprehensive approach to addressing feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when in full bloom for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the echinacea purpurea plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to pulverize the dried echinacea purpurea into a fine powder for easy capsule filling.
Fill gelatin capsules with 400-500 milligrams of the echinacea purpurea powder and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal echinacea purpurea capsules in an airtight container at room temperature to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound extracted from the bark of the Cinchona tree. This compound has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria and other febrile illnesses. The anti-inflammatory properties of quinine help to soothe the body and bring down the temperature, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with fever. It also helps to boost the immune system, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Quinoline alkaloids: These compounds help with feverish feeling by reducing the temperature and alleviating the symptoms associated with malaria, a condition often characterized by fever.
Quinic acid derivatives: These derivatives help by acting as a natural antipyretic, which means they help reduce fever and relieve associated discomfort.
Cinchonine: This alkaloid compound helps by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, which reduces swelling and relieves the body of the discomfort associated with feverish feeling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Quinine-rich leaves are commonly used to make capsules for feverish feelings due to their anti-malarial properties.
Barks: Cinchona bark is used to make capsules for feverish feelings due to its high concentration of quinine and other alkaloids.
Seeds: Cinchona seeds are sometimes used to make capsules for feverish feelings due to their presence of quinoline alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 500g of cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250g of the powder with 125g of gelatin and 5g of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Fill 2000 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with feverish feeling because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It helps to reduce inflammation and fight off the underlying infection that causes fever. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba also work to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the affected areas, helping to reduce fever and promote overall recovery. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's immune-boosting properties help to fight off the infection, alleviating symptoms and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help to reduce fever by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which can alleviate the symptoms of fever and promote overall well-being.
Bilobalide: It has a sedative effect, which can help to calm the body and mind, reducing the discomfort and anxiety associated with feverish feelings.
Ginkgolides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate the symptoms of fever, such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fever, which is attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, making them a commonly used part for treating feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 250 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba-gelatin mixture at a dosage of 120-240 mg per capsule.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata capsules helps with feverish feeling because it has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever. The active compound, andrographolide, has been shown to modulate the immune system and reduce pro-inflammatory cytokines, leading to a decrease in fever and discomfort. Additionally, Andrographis paniculata has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, making it a popular natural remedy for feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antipyretic agent, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate feverish feelings.
Andrographolide glucoside: Exhibits antipyretic and immunomodulatory properties, which help to regulate the body's response to infections and reduce fever.
Ne Andresinol: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the relief of feverish feelings and the overall reduction of inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part of Andrographis paniculata used to make capsules for feverish feelings due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a bioactive compound.
Stems: Stems of Andrographis paniculata contain a significant amount of andrographolide and are also used to make capsules for feverish feelings.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Andrographis paniculata are used to make capsules for feverish feelings as they contain a high concentration of andrographolide and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and stems and dry them in a warm place for 7 days.
Grind 200g of dried andrographis paniculata into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 50g of tapioca starch and 25g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the powder mixture and seal them properly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain licorice root, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces body heat and soothes the nervous system. The flavonoids present in licorice root have a cooling effect on the body, which helps to alleviate feverish sensations. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules are believed to have antipyretic properties, meaning they can help to regulate body temperature and provide relief from heat and discomfort associated with fever. This makes it a popular natural remedy for feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are involved in the development of fever.
Saponins: They have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate feverish feelings.
Glycyrrhizin: It has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and regulate the immune response, thereby alleviating feverish feelings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Leaves: Contain glycyrrhizin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce fever and alleviate other symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes are also a source of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that have fever-reducing properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots to obtain the desired medicinal quantity.
Grind the extracted roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 50 grams of the powdered root with 250 grams of gelatin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill 1000 empty capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine for 5 minutes.
Seal the filled capsules by pressing the two halves of the capsule together firmly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain ephedrine alkaloids, which are known for their decongestant and antipyretic properties. The herbs work by stimulating the body's thermogenesis, increasing heat production and helping to reduce fever. Additionally, ephedrine helps to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and promoting the removal of toxins from the body. This combination of actions helps to alleviate symptoms of fever, making Ephedra sinica capsules a popular natural remedy for colds, flu, and other fever-related conditions.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Helps to reduce feverish feeling by acting as an adrenergic receptor agonist, which induces vasoconstriction and increases sweating to lower body temperature.
N-Methyltyramine: Contributes to reducing fever by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which play a role in inducing fever.
Ephedradine: Exhibits antipyretic and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate fever and pain associated with feverish conditions.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make capsules due to their high concentration of ephedrine, a key ingredient in treating fever and cold symptoms.
Leaves: Utilized to make capsules due to their content of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help alleviate feverish feelings and respiratory issues.
Stems: Employed to make capsules because they contain ephedrine and other alkaloids that aid in reducing fever and relieving congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain a concentrated form of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound helps to reduce the production of mucus, making it easier to breathe. As a result, it helps to alleviate congestion, coughs, and sore throats associated with fever. Additionally, eucalyptol has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to lower body temperature and provide relief from the discomfort of a fever. It also promotes relaxation and reduces overall discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This volatile terpene has a cooling effect on the body and helps to reduce fever by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins.
Catechins: These flavonoid phenolics have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate associated symptoms such as headaches and body aches.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid phenolic, quercetin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate feverish feeling by promoting the body's natural healing processes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules due to their high content of eucalyptol, a compound that helps relieve fever and reduce inflammation.
Barks: They are used to make capsules for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues.
Stems: They are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects that help to treat feverish feelings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a healthy plant within a 6-8 hour period.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm air circulation oven at 40-50 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a grinder at a speed of 10,000 rpm.
Mix 250-500 mg of the ground eucalyptus globulus powder with 250-500 mg of a suitable capsule filler.
Fill 100-200 empty vegetable capsules with the eucalyptus globulus powder mixture and seal the capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with feverish feeling because they have been traditionally used to boost the immune system and enhance energy levels. The herb is believed to stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, helping to combat infections and reduce inflammation. It also contains antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals, promoting a sense of well-being and reducing the severity of feverish symptoms. As a result, Astragalus membranaceus capsules may provide relief from fatigue and discomfort associated with feverish feelings.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with feverish feeling by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with fever.
Saponins: Aid in reducing fever by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and promoting the elimination of pathogens, which helps to lower body temperature.
Polysaccharides: Assist in alleviating feverish feelings by enhancing the body's immune function and increasing the production of immune cells, which helps to combat infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, that help reduce fever.
Leaves: They are used because they contain astragalosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and help alleviate feverish symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus root from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Clean the root material by removing any debris or contaminants using a fine-mesh sieve.
Grind 50 grams of the cleaned root material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 250 grams of gelatin powder and 50 grams of tapioca starch to create a uniform blend.
Fill 200 gelatin capsules each with 500 milligrams of the herbal powder mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with feverish feeling because they contain high levels of antiviral compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause respiratory infections. The elderberry extract in these capsules has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate associated symptoms like headaches and body aches. By boosting the immune system and reducing viral load, Sambucus nigra capsules can provide relief from feverish feelings and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate its associated symptoms.
Phenolic acids (mainly salicylic acid): They have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve the pain and discomfort associated with fever.
Triterpenoids (mainly aucubin): They have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce the body temperature and alleviate the symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are commonly used to make capsules for feverish feelings due to their high content of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are also used, particularly in combination with flowers, to make capsules that help alleviate fever and flu symptoms.

Fruits (Berries): The berries of Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberries, are used to make capsules that help to reduce the severity and duration of feverish feelings, as they contain anthocyanins with strong antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 50c for 2 hours or until they reach a dry brittle state.
Grind 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin and add a small amount of water to facilitate binding.
Fill a capsule with the gelatin mixture and seal it using a machine or manually.
